extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won final Rome stage sprint from distance after COINS disrupted GIRMAY's intermediate sprint campaign successfully.
tactical_patterns: COINS successfully disrupted GIRMAY's intermediate sprint points accumulation throughout stage, revealing targeted denial tactics work in processional stages.
THOMAS Geraint contributed to CAVENDISH leadout despite GC secured, showing team willing to sacrifice WorldTour leader for stage glory.
Break of BENEDETTI caught with 13km remaining shows sprint teams now commit full chase resources earlier on processional stages.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: Won from distance off Colombian wheel, suggesting early kick still viable in messy Rome finale circuit
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: Pulled in leadout for teammate despite being second overall, revealing post-GC-decision team hierarchy flexibility
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: negative: Lost intermediate sprint classification to targeted denial tactics by COINS, vulnerable when isolated without leadout
